title: Sterculia australis
taxonomy: Sterculia australis is classified in the kingdom Plantae, within the family Sterculiaceae. This species is further grouped under the order Malvales and the class Magnoliopsida. The order Malvales is a group of flowering plants that includes other families such as Malvaceae and Tiliaceae. Sterculia australis is also classified as a dicotyledon, a type of plant with two cotyledons in its seedling stage. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.

intro: Sterculia australis, also known as the pink candelabra tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sterculiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Sterculia australis include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sterculia australis is often prepared as a tea or extract, and is used in the form of capsules or topical creams to leverage its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial benefits.
